stemHtml: The fundamental difference between the aerodynamic characteristics of two and three-dimensional flow is that, in a three-dimensional flow about a wing:

options.a: a spanwise component exists in addition to the chordwise speed component.
options.b: a downward component is added to the chordwise speed component of the flow behind the wing.
options.c: an upward component is added to the chordwise speed component.
options.d: flow separation occurs at the stall.
